info.name: Font
info.comment: Provides font management functionality and hosts the Font class.

description.p: The Font module maintains the system font database and provides query functions for resolving font family names, styles, file paths and metadata. Fixed-size bitmap fonts are recognised through the Windows .fon format, while TrueType fonts are scanned as scalable font resources.

Bitmap fonts can be opened and drawn directly by the Font class. Scalable TrueType rendering is handled by the Vector module and VectorText class; the Font module supplies the database information that allows those fonts to be selected.
For a thorough introduction to typesetting history and terminology as it applies to computing, we recommend visiting Google Fonts Knowledge page: https://fonts.google.com/knowledge

function.name: CharWidth
function.comment: Returns the width of a character.

input.param: The font to use for calculating the character width.

description.p: Returns the pixel width of a bitmap font character. Char is interpreted as a Unicode character value. Bitmap fonts provide a maximum of 256 glyph slots; unsupported characters fall back to the font's default character.
The font's GlyphSpacing value is not included in the returned width. If Font is NULL or has no character table, the result is 0.

function.result: The pixel width of the character, or 0 if it cannot be measured.

function.name: GetList
function.comment: Returns a list of all available system fonts.

input.param: The font list is returned here.

description.p: Returns a linked list of available system fonts. The returned list is allocated as a single resource and must be released with FreeResource() when it is no longer required.

result.error: No data is available for use
Access to the font database was denied, or the object does not exist.
AllocMemory() failed to create a new memory block

function.name: RefreshFonts
function.comment: Refreshes the system font list with up-to-date font information.

description.p: Scans the fonts: volume and rebuilds the font database.
Refreshing fonts can take an extensive amount of time because each font file must be analysed for family, style, metric and metadata information. The fonts:fonts.cfg file is rewritten on completion.

result.error: Access to the font database was denied, or the object does not exist.
Failed to read font database entries after scanning.
Failed to open fonts:fonts.cfg for writing.

function.name: ResolveFamilyName
function.comment: Convert a CSV family string to a single family name.
function.prototype: ERR fnt::ResolveFamilyName(const std::string_view & String, CSTRING * Result)

input.param: A CSV family string to resolve.
The resolved family name is returned in this parameter.

description.p: Converts a CSV family string to one resolved family name. String is parsed from left to right, with each family name or wildcard tested against the font database in order. If a single asterisk is used to terminate the list, the system default is returned when no earlier name matches.
Individual names may use the common wildcards ? and *; for example, Times New * can match Times New Roman if it is available.
The returned Result is borrowed storage. Copy it immediately if it needs to survive a later font database refresh.

result.error: It was not possible to resolve the String to a known font family.
Access to the font database was denied, or the object does not exist.
GetField() failed to retrieve a field value

function.name: SelectFont
function.comment: Searches for a 'best fitting' font file, based on family name and style.
function.prototype: ERR fnt::SelectFont(const std::string_view & Name, const std::string_view & Style, CSTRING * Path, FMETA * Meta)

input.param: The name of a font face to search for (case insensitive).
The preferred style, e.g. Bold or Italic.
The location of the best-matching font file is returned in this parameter.
Optional, returns additional meta information about the font file.

description.p: Resolves a font family Name and preferred Style to a font file path. The family name must exist in the font database. If the requested style is unavailable, the function falls back to the face's regular style or first registered style.
The returned Path is allocated and must be released with FreeResource() when it is no longer required.

result.error: Unable to find a suitable font.
Access to the font database was denied, or the object does not exist.
AllocMemory() failed to create a new memory block

function.name: StringWidth
function.comment: Returns the pixel width of any given string in relation to a font's settings.
function.prototype: INT fnt::StringWidth(objFont * Font, const std::string_view & String, INT Chars)

input.param: The maximum number of characters to measure, or -1 to measure the entire string.

description.p: Calculates the pixel width of String using the supplied Font object's current metrics and spacing settings. Line feeds are handled by measuring each line independently and returning the width of the longest line.
Word wrapping is not applied, even if WrapEdge has been set on the Font object.

function.result: The pixel width of the string, or 0 if Font is NULL, uninitialised or String is empty.

constants.comment: Universal values for alignment of graphics and text
constants.const: Align to bottom
Synonym for HORIZONTAL | VERTICAL
Align to horizontal center
Align to left
Synonym for HORIZONTAL | VERTICAL
Align to right
Align to top
Align to vertical center

constants.comment: Result flags for the SelectFont() function.
constants.const: The font should not appear in any named list shown to the user.
The Freetype hinter should be used.
The light version of the Freetype hinter should be used.
The hinting information provided by the font should be given preference.
The font is scalable (assume fixed otherwise).
This is a scalable font featuring variable metrics.
Process all characters.
Terminate operation at the first line feed or word-wrap.
The Font's Y coordinate is the base line.
Font is described as having a bold weight (read only).
Underline the font with a double-sized line, using the colour defined in Underline.
Font is described as using italics (read only).

constants.comment: Force hinting options for a font.
constants.const: The Freetype hinter will be forcibly imposed.
The light version of the Freetype hinter will be forcibly imposed.
The hinting information provided by the font will be given preference.

struct.name: FontList
struct.comment: For GetList(), describes a system font.
struct.field: Pointer to the next entry in the list.
The name of the font face.
Reference to another font Name if this is an alias.
Pointer to an array of fixed point sizes supported by the font.
Supported styles are listed here in CSV format.
For variable fonts, lists all supported axis codes in CSV format
TRUE if the font is scalable.
TRUE if the font has variable metrics.
TRUE if the font should be hidden from user font lists.
